translated: Jacob lived in the land where his father had sojourned, which was the land of Canaan.

ref: which showed that Joseph was his favorite son, and so Joseph's brothers hated him and would not be friendly to him.
text: Na ne nuanom hunuu sɛ wɔn agya dɔ no sene wɔn nyinaa no, wɔtanee no, na wɔmfa abodwoɔ ne no nkasa.
translated: And his brothers saw that their father loved him more than all of them, they hated him, and they could not speak peacefully with him.

ref: his father Jacob said to him, “I want you to go to your brothers. They are with the sheep near Shechem.” “Yes, sir,” Joseph answered.
text: Na Israel ka kyerɛɛ Yosef sɛ: Sɛ wonim sɛ wo nuanom rewɛn mmoa wɔ Sekem. Bra na mensoma wo wɔn nkyɛn. Na ɔse: Menie.
translated: And Israel said to Joseph: You know that your brothers are tending the flock in Shechem. Come, and I will send you to them. And he said: Here I am.

ref: Reuben heard this and tried to protect Joseph from them. “Let's not kill him,” he said.
text: Na Ruben teeɛ no, ɔgyee no firii wɔn nsam kaa sɛ: Mommma yɛnnku no.
translated: When Reuben heard this, he rescued him from their hands, saying, "Let us not kill him.

ref: “Don't murder him or even harm him. Just throw him into a well out here in the desert.” Reuben planned to rescue Joseph later and take him back to his father.
text: Na ɔkaa sɛ: Monnhwie mogya nngu, na momfa no nto ɛserɛ so ha abura yi mu; na mommfa mo nsa nnka no. Ɔreyɛ na anka wayi no afiri wɔn nsam de no asane akɔma n’agya.
translated: And he said: Do not shed blood, but take him and throw him into this pit here in the wilderness; and do not lay a hand on him. He was doing this so that he could rescue him from their hands and return him to his father.

ref: When Joseph came to his brothers, they pulled off his fancy coat
text: Na Yosef duruu ne nuanom nkyɛn no, wɔworɔɔ n’atadeɛ ne batakari fɛfɛ a ɔhyɛ no,
translated: And when Joseph reached his brothers, they stripped him of his tunic and the beautiful robe he was wearing.

ref: When Reuben returned to the well and did not find Joseph there, he tore his clothes in sorrow.
text: Na Ruben sane baa abura no so, na hwɛ, na Yosef nni abura no mu. Na ɔsunsuanee ne ntadeɛ mu,
translated: And Reuben returned to the well, and behold, Joseph was not in the well. And he tore his clothes,

ref: The third one was born when Judah was in Chezib, and she named him Shelah.
text: Na ɔnyinsɛnee bio woo ɔbabarima, na ɔtoo no din Sela. Na ɔwoo no no, na ɔwɔ Kesib.
translated: And she conceived again and bore a son, and she named him Sela. When she bore him, she was in Kesib.

ref: The Lord wasn't pleased with Onan and took his life too.
text: Na deɛ ɔyɛeɛ no yɛɛ bɔne wɔ AWURADE anim, enti ɔkumm ɔno nso.
translated: What he did was evil in the sight of the LORD, so he killed him also.

ref: Judah did not want the same thing to happen to his son Shelah, and he told Tamar, “Go home to your father and live there as a widow until my son Shelah is grown.” So Tamar went to live with her father.
text: Na afei Yuda ka kyerɛɛ n’ase Tamar sɛ: Kɔtena kuna mu wɔ w’agya fie kɔsi sɛ me ba Sela bɛnyini. Ɛfiri sɛ ɔse, anyɛ saa a, ɔno nso bɛwu sɛ ne nuanom. Na Tamar kɔ kɔtenaa n’agya fie.
translated: And now Judah said to his daughter-in-law Tamar: Go and live as a widow in your father's house until my son Shelah grows up. For he said, otherwise, he too would die like his brothers. And Tamar went and lived in her father's house.

ref: and asked her to sleep with him. She asked, “What will you give me if I do?”
text: Na ɔmane kɔɔ ne nkyɛn ɔkwan no ho kaa sɛ: Ma mene wo nkɔda ɛ, nso na ɔnnim sɛ n’ase a. Na ɔse: Ɛdeɛn na wobɛma me na wone me ada?
translated: And he went to her by the road saying: Let me sleep with you, but he did not know that she was his daughter-in-law. And she said: What will you give me so

ref: Tamar later gave birth to twins. But before either of them was born, one of them stuck a hand out of her womb. The woman who was helping tied a red thread around the baby's hand and explained, “This one came out first.”
text: Na ɔrewoɔ no, ɔbaako yii ne nsa, na awogyefoɔ no sɔɔ mu de asaawa kɔkɔɔ kyekyeree ne nsa no kaa sɛ: Oyi na ɔfirii adi kane.
translated: And as she was giving birth, one (baby) put out its hand, and the midwives grasped it and tied its hand with a red thread, saying: This is the one who came out first.

ref: and Potiphar's wife soon noticed him. She asked him to make love to her,
text: Na ɛbaa sɛ akyire yi no, Yosef wura yere tuu n’ani sii ne so, na ɔkaa sɛ: Wo ne me mmɛda.
translated: And it came to pass that later, Joseph's master's wife set her eyes on him, and she said: Lie with me.

ref: but he refused and said, “My master isn't worried about anything in his house, because he has placed me in charge of everything he owns.
text: Nanso wampene, na ɔka kyerɛɛ ne wura yere no sɛ: Hwɛ, sɛ me wura anya me nti ɔnhwɛ deɛ ɛwɔ ne fie, na ɔde deɛ ɔwɔ nyinaa ahyɛ me nsa.
translated: But he refused, and he said to his master's wife: Look, because of me, my master does not concern himself with what is in his house, and he has put everything

ref: No one in my master's house is more important than I am. The only thing he hasn't given me is you, and that's because you are his wife. I won't sin against God by doing such a terrible thing as this.”
text: Obiara nyɛ kɛseɛ nsene me efie ha, na ɔnkame me biribiara gye wo, ɛnam sɛ ne yere ne woɔ ntira. Na adɛn na menyɛ amumuyɛ kɛseɛ yi, na menyɛ bɔne mentia Onyankopɔn?
translated: No one is greater than me in this house, and he has withheld nothing from me except you, because you are his wife. So why should I do this great wickedness, and commit sin against God?

ref: One day, Joseph went to Potiphar's house to do his work, and none of the other servants were there.
text: Na da koro bi a ɔkɔɔ ɛdan no mu sɛ ɔrekɔyɛ n’adwuma, na efie hɔfoɔ no mu biara nni hɔ no,
translated: And one day, when he went into the house to do his work, and no one from the household was there,

ref: But when I screamed for help, he left his coat and ran out of the house.”
text: na memaa me nne so meteaa mu no, ɔgyaa n’atadeɛ wɔ me nsam, na ɔdwane firii adie.
translated: And as I cried out, he left his garment in my hands, and he fled.

ref: and threw Joseph in the same prison where the king's prisoners were kept. While Joseph was in prison,
text: Na Yosef wura no faa no de no kɔtoo afiase, deɛ ɔhene nneduafoɔ guo, na ɔdaa afiase hɔ. Onyankopɔn di Yosef akyi
translated: And Joseph's master took him and put him in prison, where the king's prisoners were kept, and he stayed there in prison. God was with Joseph.

ref: One night each of the two men had a dream, but their dreams had different meanings.
text: Na Misraimhene nsahyɛni ne ne paanootoni a na wɔgu afiase hɔ no baanu nyinaa sosoo daeɛ anadwo korɔ, ɛsono oyi daeɛ ne n’asekyerɛ, na ɛsono oyi nso daeɛ.
translated: And the king of Egypt's cupbearer and his baker, who were in prison, both dreamed a dream on the same night, each having a different dream with its own interpretation.

ref: with three branches. As soon as it budded, it blossomed, and its grapes became ripe.
text: na bobe no apae nkorabata mmiɛnsa, na ayɛ sɛdeɛ ɛrefefɛ, na anyini agu nhyerɛne, na abɔ esia ayɛ aba a abereɛ.
translated: and the vine split into three branches, and it blossomed, and grew into flowers, and bore ripe fruit.

ref: But when these good things happen, please don't forget to tell the king about me, so I can get out of this place.
text: Nanso sɛ ɛyɛ a, kae me, na sɛ ɛsi wo yie a, yɛ me adɔeɛ, na bɔ me din kyerɛ Farao, na bɛyi me ɛdan yi mu,
translated: But if it happens, remember me, and when things go well for you, show me kindness, and mention my name to Pharaoh, and bring me out of this prison.

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three baskets are three days,
text: Na Yosef buaa sɛ: Ne nkyerɛaseɛ nie: Nkyerɛnkyɛ mmiɛnsa no yɛ nnansa;
translated: And Joseph answered, saying: This is its interpretation: The three branches are three days;

ref: and in three days the king will cut off your head. He will hang your body on a pole, and birds will come and peck at it.
text: na aka nnansa na Farao ayi wo afiri ha, na wakɔsɛne wo dua so ma nnomaa adi wo nam afiri wo ho.
translated: And in three days Pharaoh will take you from here, and hang you on a tree, and birds will eat your flesh off you.

ref: and had the cook put to death. Everything happened just as Joseph had said it would,
text: Na paanootofoɔ panin no deɛ, ɔsɛnee no, sɛdeɛ Yosef kyerɛɛ wɔn aseɛ no.
translated: And as for the chief baker, he was hanged, just as Joseph had interpreted to them.

ref: The king went back to sleep and had another dream. This time seven full heads of grain were growing on a single stalk.
text: Na ɔsane soo daeɛ bio, na hwɛ, ayuo mmɛtem nson a ɛtoatoa soɔ na abɔ ma refifiri wɔ nan korɔ so.
translated: And he dreamt again, and behold, seven plump ears of corn sprouted and grew on one stalk.

ref: The thin heads of grain swallowed the seven full heads. Again the king woke up, and it had only been a dream.
text: Na mmɛtem nkorowankorowa no memenee mmɛtem nson a ɛtoatoa soɔ na ayuo wɔ mu no. Na Farao nyanee, na hwɛ, ɛyɛ daeɛ.
translated: And the thin ears of corn swallowed the seven successive ears that were full of grain. And Pharaoh woke up, and behold, it was a dream.

ref: The king's personal servant said: Now I remember what I was supposed to do.
text: Ɛnna nsahyɛfoɔ mu panin no ka kyerɛɛ Farao sɛ: Makae me mfomsoɔ nnɛ.
translated: Then the chief cupbearer said to Pharaoh: I remember my offense today.

ref: and everything happened just as he said it would. I got my job back, and the cook was put to death.
text: Na sɛdeɛ ɔkyerɛɛ yɛn aseɛ no, saa ara na ɛbaeɛ. Wɔde me sane kɔdii me dibea, na ɔno deɛ, wɔsɛnee no.
translated: And just as he explained to us, so it came to pass. They reinstated me to my position, but as for him, they disgraced him.
